title: Fix version scheme for releases inside the copr repository
body:
### Issue Description

When I go to https://copr.fedorainfracloud.org/coprs/rhcontainerbot/podman-next/package/podman/

I see package version being `4.5.0.rc1` for Podman but currently podman HEAD is set to `4.7.0-dev` so I would expect to see this version



### Steps to reproduce the issue

Go to https://copr.fedorainfracloud.org/coprs/rhcontainerbot/podman-next/package/podman/

see version



### Describe the results you received

![image](https://github.com/containers/podman/assets/436777/551c3056-72ba-4cbc-a2cc-7fd1f903d65c)


### Describe the results you expected

would expect 4.7.0-dev and not 4.5.0-RC1

it seems it's related to `git describe` command

### podman info output

```yaml
related to CI
```


### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body: @ashley-cui @mheon would be good to fix `git describe` for this. But given we need to handle https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/19447 for the release string in packit, `git describe` is not a blocker as far as packit builds go.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
